content: All interfaces that represent a concrete implementation (e.g., adapters, repositories, services) MUST also generate a mock implementation that complies with the `github.com/stretchr/testify/mock` library.
For every interface, generate a corresponding mock struct in a shared `testmock` or `mock` package at the project root (recommended for reusability), or in the same package if only used locally.
All interface methods must be implemented on the mock, delegating to `mock.Mock` methods (e.g., `Called`, `On`, `Return`).
The mock should be named with the `Mock` prefix or suffix (e.g., `MockAdapter`, `UserRepositoryMock`).
The mock must be suitable for use in table-driven tests and testify suites.
Place all mocks in a shared `testmock` or `mock` package for reusability and maintainability.
Use mocks in unit tests to isolate dependencies and assert interactions.
Document the purpose and usage of each mock.
If a mock is only used in one package, it can be placed locally, but prefer centralization for shared interfaces.
